edges.description: Loss of MGAT2 activity prevents normal maturation of complex N-glycans.
Incomplete complex N-glycan maturation produces abnormally glycosylated serum transferrin and serum N-glycan profiles.
Impaired complex N-glycan maturation is associated with severe neurological manifestations, including developmental disability, hypotonia, and epilepsy.
Defective glycan maturation perturbs lymphocyte activation and proliferation.
intermediate_mechanisms: abnormal complex N-glycans on lymphocyte mitogen-binding glycoproteins

edges.description: Episodic asystole and cardiac arrhythmia can occur as part of the expanded MGAT2-CDG phenotype.
MGAT2 was reported among CDG genes associated with nonimmune hydrops fetalis in a systematic review.
Feeding difficulty and faltering growth can occur as part of the multisystem MGAT2-CDG presentation.
Bilateral sensorineural hearing loss was reported in the expanded MGAT2-CDG clinical spectrum.
Scoliosis and kyphosis were reported as musculoskeletal features in the expanded MGAT2-CDG clinical spectrum.
Chronic respiratory failure with nocturnal ventilatory support was reported in the expanded MGAT2-CDG clinical spectrum.
Abnormal serum glycoprotein N-glycosylation is detected as a type II carbohydrate-deficient transferrin profile.
Profound global developmental disability is part of the neurological spectrum.
Hypotonia is part of the neurological spectrum.
Early-onset epilepsy is part of the neurological spectrum.
Episodic asystole is a severe arrhythmic manifestation.
The immunologic branch includes persistent hypogammaglobulinemia alongside defective proliferative responses.
Impaired cellular immune responses were evaluated after several viral respiratory infections in the reported MGAT2-CDG case.
The type II CDT pattern reports incomplete serum glycoprotein N-glycosylation caused by impaired MGAT2-dependent glycan maturation.

nodes.description: MGAT2 deficiency causes incomplete processing of serum glycoprotein N-glycans, producing abnormal transferrin glycoforms with minimal fully glycosylated protein and a type II carbohydrate-deficient transferrin pattern.

evidence.snippet: Of the biochemical testing, only the CDT profile was abnormal, revealing a marked increase of abnormally glycosylated transferrin with incomplete glycan processing and minimal amounts of the fully glycosylated protein.

Serum N-glycan profiling substantiated this result and demonstrated similarly abnormal N-linked glycosylation on serum proteins compared with an unaffected individual (Figure 1c,d; Li, Raihan, Reynoso, & He, 20i5).

nodes.description: Scoliosis and kyphosis have been reported among musculoskeletal features in MGAT2-CDG.

evidence.snippet: Subsequent medical concerns for this patient have included cortical blindness with persistent pupillary membranes, bilateral sensorineural hearing loss (mild to moderate in her right ear, moderate to moderately severe in her left ear), chronic respiratory failure with nocturnal BiPAP dependence, cyanotic episodes possibly secondary to excessive secretions leading to aspiration and bronchospasm, delayed gastric emptying with gastroesophageal reflux disease, gastrojejunostomy tube dependence, global developmental disability, diffuse hypotonia, seizures, scoliosis, kyphosis, and mildly decreased factor XI (Table S1).

nodes.description: Cardiac rhythm disturbance, including episodic asystole, can occur as part of the multisystem phenotype.

evidence.snippet: In addition to clinical features previously described in MGAT2-CDG, she experienced episodic asystole, persistent hypogammaglobulinemia, and defective ex vivo mitogen and antigen proliferative responses, but intact specific vaccine antibody titers.
MGAT2-CDG shows a type II carbohydrate-deficient transferrin profile (CDT) and is confirmed with molecular testing (Tan et al., 1996; Chang et al., 2018; Cormier-Daire et al., 2000).

readouts.interpretation: The type II CDT pattern reports incomplete serum glycoprotein N-glycosylation caused by impaired MGAT2-dependent glycan maturation.

nodes.description: MGAT2-CDG can include cardiac rhythm instability, including episodic asystole requiring pacemaker placement.

evidence.snippet: This clinical report of MGAT2-CDG highlights a novel pathogenic variant in MGAT2, c.1006_1009delGACA, and expands the phenotype to include cardiac arrhythmia, specifically episodic asystole requiring epicardial pacemaker placement, and combined immunodeficiency.

nodes.description: Persistent hypogammaglobulinemia is part of the expanded immunologic phenotype of MGAT2-CDG.

evidence.snippet: In addition to clinical features previously described in MGAT2-CDG, she experienced episodic asystole, persistent hypogammaglobulinemia, and defective ex vivo mitogen and antigen proliferative responses, but intact specific vaccine antibody titers.

nodes.description: Persistent feeding difficulties with faltering growth can require gastrostomy support in MGAT2-CDG.

evidence.snippet: Around 6 months of age, she had a gastrostomy tube placed due to persistent feeding difficulties and faltering growth.

nodes.description: Generalized hypotonia is a core early neurologic manifestation.

evidence.snippet: MGAT2-CDG (CDG-IIa) is a congenital disorder of glycosylation (CDG) associated with profound global developmental disability, hypotonia, early onset epilepsy, and other multisystem manifestations.

nodes.description: Profound developmental impairment is one of the defining neurologic features of MGAT2-CDG.

evidence.snippet: MGAT2-CDG (CDG-IIa) is a congenital disorder of glycosylation (CDG) associated with profound global developmental disability, hypotonia, early onset epilepsy, and other multisystem manifestations.

nodes.description: Failure to generate mature complex N-glycans perturbs glycoprotein-dependent neural and immune-system functions and drives the multisystem phenotype.

evidence.snippet: Complex N-glycans are essential for immune system functionality, but only one individual with MGAT2-CDG has been described to have an abnormal immunologic evaluation.

nodes.description: Loss of mature complex N-glycans impairs immune-system function, including mitogen- and antigen-driven lymphocyte proliferative responses.

evidence.snippet: Here, we present a patient with MGAT2-CDG with both persistent hypogammaglobulinemia and T-cell proliferation abnormalities.

nodes.description: Intravenous immunoglobulin replacement was used to manage persistent hypogammaglobulinemia in the reported MGAT2-CDG case.

evidence.snippet: she was started on intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIG) replacement therapy at 2 years 8 months old.
MGAT2-CDG, previously known as CDG-IIa, is an autosomal recessive CDG with biallelic pathogenic variants in MGAT2.
MGAT2 | HGNC:7045 | MGAT2-congenital disorder of glycosylation | MONDO:0008908 | AR | Moderate

nodes.description: Loss of MGAT2 activity disrupts a critical glycosyltransferase step in the conversion of oligomannose glycans to mature complex N-glycans.

evidence.snippet: Human MGAT2 encodes N-acetylglucosaminyltransferase II, which is a critical enzyme in the processing of oligomannose to complex N-glycans.

nodes.description: MGAT2-CDG includes severe neurological involvement with profound global developmental disability, hypotonia, and early-onset epilepsy.

evidence.snippet: MGAT2-CDG (CDG-IIa) is a congenital disorder of glycosylation (CDG) associated with profound global developmental disability, hypotonia, early onset epilepsy, and other multisystem manifestations.

nodes.description: Prenatal presentation with nonimmune hydrops fetalis has been reported in a minority of MGAT2-CDG cases within the broader CDG literature.

evidence.snippet: The genes reported for CDG with NIHF for 15 distinct families include: PMM2 in 47% (7/15), ALG9 in 20% (3/15), ALG8 in 13% (2/15), ALG1 in 7% (1/15), MGAT2 in 7% (1/15), and COG6 7% (1/15).

nodes.description: Several viral respiratory infections prompted detailed immunologic evaluation in the expanded MGAT2-CDG case.

evidence.snippet: Detailed immunologic evaluation was performed at 2 years of age following several viral respiratory infections, including human meta-pneumovirus, rhinovirus/enterovirus, and parainfluenza type 3.

nodes.description: Chronic respiratory failure with nocturnal BiPAP dependence has been reported in the expanded MGAT2-CDG phenotype.

evidence.snippet: Subsequent medical concerns for this patient have included cortical blindness with persistent pupillary membranes, bilateral sensorineural hearing loss (mild to moderate in her right ear, moderate to moderately severe in her left ear), chronic respiratory failure with nocturnal BiPAP dependence, cyanotic episodes possibly secondary to excessive secretions leading to aspiration and bronchospasm, delayed gastric emptying with gastroesophageal reflux disease, gastrojejunostomy tube dependence, global developmental disability, diffuse hypotonia, seizures, scoliosis, kyphosis, and mildly decreased factor XI (Table S1).

nodes.description: Early-onset epilepsy is part of the recurrent neurologic phenotype.

evidence.snippet: MGAT2-CDG (CDG-IIa) is a congenital disorder of glycosylation (CDG) associated with profound global developmental disability, hypotonia, early onset epilepsy, and other multisystem manifestations.

nodes.description: Bilateral sensorineural hearing loss has been reported in the expanded MGAT2-CDG phenotype.

evidence.snippet: Subsequent medical concerns for this patient have included cortical blindness with persistent pupillary membranes, bilateral sensorineural hearing loss (mild to moderate in her right ear, moderate to moderately severe in her left ear), chronic respiratory failure with nocturnal BiPAP dependence, cyanotic episodes possibly secondary to excessive secretions leading to aspiration and bronchospasm, delayed gastric emptying with gastroesophageal reflux disease, gastrojejunostomy tube dependence, global developmental disability, diffuse hypotonia, seizures, scoliosis, kyphosis, and mildly decreased factor XI (Table S1).

nodes.description: Trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole prophylaxis was used after defective lymphocyte proliferative responses were identified in the reported case.

evidence.snippet: Given the poor proliferative responses, she was started on trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole prophylaxis.
